metadata.quality_assurance: All YouTube videos verified as professional PT content - educational/institutional license
metadata.animation_system: AI-Generated Medical 3D - Commissioned for clinical accuracy

exercises.clinical_notes: Isometric quadriceps activation with dynamic hip flexion. Optimal for early-stage post-op rehabilitation. Maintain neutral spine and avoid hip external rotation.

animation.posture_guidance: supine position, neutral spine alignment, knee locked in extension, hip flexion to 45-60 degrees ROM
key_movements: Starting position: supine, legs extended, feet hip-width apart
Activation phase: tighten quadriceps muscle (hold 2 seconds)
Raise phase: lift leg 12-18 inches off surface (2 second duration)
Hold phase: maintain position 1 second
Lower phase: controlled descent (2 second duration)
Rest phase: return to starting position

exercises.clinical_notes: Isometric strengthening with gravity assistance. Ideal for loading tolerance progression. Control knee tracking over toes to prevent valgus strain.

animation.background: neutral clinical white with wall reference
animation.posture_guidance: back against wall, knees at 60-90 degree flexion, neutral spine, weight distributed evenly through feet
key_movements: Starting position: back to wall, feet 12 inches from wall, hip-width apart
Descent phase: slide back down wall (3 second duration)
Hold phase: maintain position at 60-90 degree knee flexion (30-45 seconds)
Ascent phase: press through heels, slide back up wall (3 second duration)
Rest phase: return to starting position

exercises.assigned_reps_sets: 3 sets × 12-15 reps (standing variant) or 2 sets × 10-12 reps (lying variant)
exercises.clinical_notes: Standing variant offers proprioceptive benefit; prone variant isolates hamstring more effectively. Progress standing to prone as strength improves. Maintain neutral spine throughout.

animation.posture_guidance: standing position with neutral spine, slight knee bend in planted leg, hamstring ROM 0-90 degrees flexion
key_movements: Starting position: standing, hands on support, feet hip-width apart
Engagement phase: activate core, maintain neutral spine (1 second)
Curl phase: bend knee, bringing heel toward buttocks (2 second duration)
Hold phase: maintain knee flexion position (1 second)
Extension phase: controlled return to starting position (2 second duration)
Rest phase: return to neutral stance

exercises.clinical_notes: Static stretch - no ballistic bouncing. Hold at point of mild tension, never pain. Perform daily for best ROM outcomes. Can be performed against wall or using step.

animation.background: neutral clinical white with wall reference
animation.posture_guidance: standing position facing wall, forward leg knee flexed to 20 degrees, rear leg knee extended, heel in contact with floor
key_movements: Starting position: standing arm's length from wall, hands on wall at shoulder height
Lunge setup: step right foot forward, knee slightly flexed
Position setup: extend left leg behind, heel flat on ground
Hold position: lean upper body forward into wall (no bouncing)
Optimal stretch: feel gentle stretch in left calf muscle (30-60 seconds)
Release: step back to neutral position

exercises.assigned_reps_sets: 3 sets × 15-20 reps (10 seconds hold per rep)
medical_contraindications: Severe acute knee swelling (aspirate first if necessary)
exercises.clinical_notes: Isometric exercise safe immediately post-surgery. Essential for early quad activation and prevention of atrophy. Can perform 4-6x daily in early rehab phase.

animation.posture_guidance: seated position with legs extended, neutral spine, small rolled towel under knee for visual feedback
key_movements: Starting position: seated, legs extended, back supported, pillow under knee
Engagement phase: tighten muscle on top of thigh (quadriceps)
Hold phase: maintain maximum muscle contraction (10 seconds)
Observation: knee should straighten slightly, kneecap should pull upward
Release phase: relax quadriceps (2 second rest)
Recovery: rest between repetitions

exercises.assigned_reps_sets: 3 sets × 10-15 repetitions each direction (forward-back, side-to-side, circular)
medical_contraindications: Nerve root compression with severe pain
exercises.clinical_notes: Gravity-assisted pendulum motion. Safe for immediate post-op shoulder. Progress from small circles to larger ROM as pain-free motion improves. Can perform multiple times daily.

animation.posture_guidance: forward bend position with neutral spine, affected arm relaxed and hanging, small oscillating movements
key_movements: Starting position: standing, feet shoulder-width apart, slight forward bend (30 degrees)
Arm setup: affected arm hangs freely, other hand on table or support
Forward-back motion: allow gravity to swing arm forward and backward; 10-15 swings
Side-to-side motion: swing arm across body and out to side; 10-15 swings
Circular motion: perform small clockwise circles, then counter-clockwise; 10 circles each direction
Rest: return to standing, allow arm to rest at side

youtube_reference.clinical_focus: Shoulder mobility and early post-op rehabilitation

safety_checks_performed: ROM validation against standard rehabilitation protocols
Contraindication review against common orthopedic diagnoses
PT credentials verification for all video channels
important_notes: Doctor prescriptions take absolute priority - only modify reps/sets on doctor's order
Animation metadata prescribes ideal form - not substitute for live PT supervision
YouTube videos for reference only - not substitute for personalized PT assessment
All exercises should be adapted to individual patient tolerance
Pain response monitoring is essential - STOP if pain exceeds baseline
